entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: Maize germplasm representing temperate, tropical and subtropical regions used in the study

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: Environmental stress that delays developmental events and reduces plant height and biomass

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: A secondary trait used as a criterion for drought tolerance in maize

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: Above-ground biomass measured by NDVI, used as a secondary trait for drought tolerance

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural output of maize, which is reduced due to inadequate photosynthate under drought

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of maize to maintain growth and yield under water-stressed conditions

entities.name: NDVI
entities.description: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index used to measure biomass at different stages

entities.name: SNP
entities.description: Single nucleotide polymorphism markers used for genotyping and LD mapping

entities.name: HAPLOTYPE
entities.description: A combination of two or more SNPs used for linkage disequilibrium or association mapping

entities.name: LD MAPPING
entities.description: Linkage disequilibrium mapping used to identify QTLs for plant height and biomass

entities.name: ASSOCIATION MAPPING
entities.description: A genetic mapping method used to identify associations between markers and traits

entities.name: QTL
entities.description: Quantitative trait loci identified for plant height and biomass

entities.name: WELL-WATERED
entities.description: A water regime used as a control condition for measuring plant traits

entities.name: WATER-STRESSED
entities.description: A water regime used to evaluate drought tolerance and identify QTLs

entities.name: CHROMOSOMAL REGIONS
entities.description: Genomic areas defined by haplotype loci

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC VARIATION
entities.description: The observable variation in traits explained by SNP and haplotype loci
